MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth C. Sears, Esq., of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party NACCO Materials Handling Group, Inc.
Christopher Hersom, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party James Truitt Jr.

RULING:
DEFENDANT NACCO MATERIALS HANDLING GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant NACCO Materials Handling Group, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Truitt was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Because defendant did not submit the special interrogatories it propounded on plaintiffs as part of its motion papers, the court is unable to determine what questions plaintiffs were responding to and and thus whether plaintiffs' responses are 'factually devoid' as that term is used in Andrews, Union Bank and other summary judgment appellate decisions.. Further, Mr. Truitt's deposition testimony precludes defendant from sustaining its burden that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to Hyster-supplied (OEM) replacement vehicle parts. Defendant's separate statements did not make an issue of whether those parts contained asbestos, and whether such exposure was a substantial factor. Defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their claim for false representation and request for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
